QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $713.29 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Markets closed mixed Monday as Trump rejected an Iranian peace proposal, sending WTI crude back toward $100 and gold to a new all-time high above $4,700. The S&P 500 eked out a record led by semiconductor names, though the rally is narrow and overlaid with fresh trade war fears after the White House threatened 100% tariffs on Chinese imports and expanded export restrictions. Tuesday’s CPI report (08:30 ET) will test the soft-landing narrative amid rising energy costs and a hawkish Fed holding rates at 3.50-3.75%.
commodities_note: WTI -0.93% to $99.01, pulling back from Monday's open spike as Hormuz ceasefire hopes collapsed. Gold +1.12% to $4,739.40 as geopolitical hedging intensified. The US 10-year yield rose 1.05% to 4.41%, reflecting sticky inflation expectations ahead of CPI.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks grind higher despite unresolved NIM compression. UOB upgraded to WATCH on technicals (R:R 2.5:1), but structural relief requires US10Y <4.20% or MAS easing — neither in sight. DBS and OCBC remain hard-blocked on extension; the cluster is tactically mixed and awaiting the next rate signal. Next catalyst: MAS October 2026 policy window.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired by dual NIM compression channels; UOB tactical divergence
singapore.key_development: U11_SI upgraded to WATCH on improved R:R, but US10Y 4.41% sustains the USD-SGD funding-cost headwind for the entire cluster.

semiconductors.summary: Semis are uniformly extended, with 9 of 11 members hard-blocked above 5% extension. The AI supercycle is structurally intact — CoWoS sold out, HBM3E supply constrained through 2027, and hyperscaler capex backlogs growing — but entry levels are absent. TSEM reports tomorrow as the first near-term catalyst; the $256 bull case requires guidance acceleration that clears the priced-for-perfection bar. LITE's +16.5% gap triggers RCP review; the NVDA photonics investment is confirmed but consolidation is required.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extreme — no clean entries across the cluster
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX +2.06% to 532.76 (record); LITE surged 16.8% on photonics demand signals; TSEM priced for perfection ahead of May 13 earnings.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster remains structurally bullish but tactically extreme. GOOG and AMZN are >15% above 50-day averages on AI capex optimism, with entry gated on pullbacks to $320-$340 and $230-$240 respectively. MSFT holds near the 50-day with a cleaner fundamental read after the OpenAI revenue cap. PLTR's 66x PE remains a valuation overhang despite the pullback; a reclaim of the SMA50 would signal the next entry trigger.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended across mega-caps
big_tech.key_development: MSFT benefits from OpenAI revenue cap at $38B, reducing concentration risk; AMZN and GOOG extreme extension persists with no entry signals.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment alongside LITE. Optical interconnects are the bandwidth bottleneck for GPU clusters beyond 100k GPUs.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 15-20% from current levels, aligning with LITE's RCP consolidation pattern.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Primary supplier of NVDA Spectrum-X backend networking switches. AI datacenter scale drives 800G/1.6T transceiver demand that overlaps with LITE and NOK.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after NVDA May 21 earnings for networking share-of-wallet confirmation.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO; hyperscaler capex directly routes through MRVL's custom silicon design wins for Amazon and Google.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO June 4 earnings confirm custom ASIC market growth above 40% YoY.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: HBM3E duopoly risk for SK Hynix and Micron; Samsung's HBM qualification progress is the structural bear case for HBM ASPs.
interconnected.entry_note: Scout on any Samsung HBM3E qualification announcement that threatens the SK Hynix/Micron duopoly.

interconnected.name: Exxon Mobil
interconnected.reason: Direct beneficiary of sustained Hormuz-driven oil dislocation; WTI at $99.01 and gold at ATH signal persistent geopolitical premium.
interconnected.entry_note: Worth adding to watchlist if WTI sustains above $100 into Q3 2026, confirming structural supply deficit.

active_risks: Hormuz blockade escalates: Trump rejects Iran peace proposal, US-Iran exchange fire, ceasefire hopes collapse. Oil supply disrupted, WTI near $100.
US-China trade war intensifies: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on Chinese imports and new export restrictions; China retaliates with rare earth controls and port fees.
Semiconductor supply chain fragmentation: US export controls on advanced chips expand; MATCH Act threatens ASML EUV/DUV sales to China. Supply chain uncertainty intensifies.
Fed policy paralysis: Core PCE at 3.0% with rising energy costs; Fed holds rates at 3.50-3.75% with no cut signals, stalling multiple expansion for growth stocks.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil elevated from Hormuz disruption sustains energy-cost inflation, keeping the Fed on hold and US10Y above 4.40% — which directly compresses Singapore bank NIMs and caps tech multiple expansion. Semis rally despite trade-war escalation, driven by AI supply constraints that override tariff fears for now. Gold at ATH reflects persistent geopolitical hedging.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump rejects Iran peace proposal, calling response 'totally unacceptable'; US threatens 100% tariffs on Chinese imports and expanded export restrictions. Microsoft OpenAI partnership restructured with $38B revenue cap.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries; the watchlist is uniformly extended and CPI on Tuesday could catalyze a volatility spike. Wait for pullbacks to defined 50-day levels before deploying capital.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 21 names are hard-blocked on technical extension or priced for perfection; zero BUY or ACCUMULATE signals. The watchlist offers no clean entry points — wait for pullbacks or post-earnings resets.
